title: aegilops mutica subsp. loliacea
intro: Aegilops mutica subsp. loliacea, also known as a wild grass or a type of goat grass, is a plant species that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Aegilops mutica subsp. loliacea include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. It can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal usage.
